name: Logan County School Employees
description_short: Logan County School Employees Federal Credit Union is an NCUA-insured credit union serving school employees in Logan, WV since 1985, offering savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans.
description_long: Logan County School Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1985 and is headquartered in Logan, West Virginia. As a federally chartered credit union with NCUA insurance protection, it serves a specific member base of school employees in Logan County. The institution operates with approximately $1.23 million in total assets, $0.98 million in deposits, and $0.19 million in outstanding loans, reflecting its role as a community-focused financial cooperative. The credit union offers a range of standard banking and lending products. Members can access savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 1 to 5 years, mortgage products for both home purchases and refinances (30-year and 15-year options), home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, personal loans, and other lending services. The institution advertises competitive rates including a 4.59% APY on 1-year CDs, 4.00% APY on 2 and 5-year CDs, and mortgage rates around 4.91% for 30-year fixed and 4.39% for 15-year fixed options. What distinguishes Logan County School Employees FCU is its membership-focused mission serving school district employees specifically. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, it operates on a cooperative, member-owned model where profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders. The institution maintains NCUA federal insurance, providing deposit protection up to standard limits. Its long operational history since 1985 demonstrates stability within its local community. A significant limitation is the availability of current rate and product information. The website clearly states "We do not yet have rates for Logan County School Employees" for both deposit and loan products as of April 7, 2026, directing prospective members to contact the institution directly. With only $1.23 million in assets, this is a very small credit union with likely limited branch locations and possibly restricted service availability. Potential members should verify current offerings and eligibility requirements directly with the credit union, as the publicly available information on this profile site is incomplete.
pros: NCUA federally insured deposits, protecting up to $250,000 per depositor
Member-owned credit union structure returning profits to account holders
Established since 1985, demonstrating 40+ years of operational stability
Offers full range of consumer banking products including savings, CDs, mortgages, and personal loans
Serves school employees specifically, creating targeted community focus
Competitive advertised rates: 4.59% APY on 1-year CDs and sub-5% mortgage rates
cons: Current rate information incomplete and unavailable on the profile site; requires direct contact with credit union
Very small asset base of $1.23 million limits service capacity and technology infrastructure
Geographically restricted to Logan County, WV area; likely no online banking or remote membership
Membership eligibility restricted to school employees, excluding general public
Limited branch network (exact number not specified, but implied to be minimal given asset size)
best_for: School employees in Logan County, West Virginia seeking community banking services
Local members looking for personalized service and member-ownership benefits
Individuals seeking NCUA-insured savings with competitive CD rates
diagnosis: Logan County School Employees FCU is best suited for school district employees in Logan, West Virginia who prioritize member-ownership benefits and community banking over digital convenience. However, prospective members must contact the credit union directly to obtain current rates and verify membership eligibility, as publicly available information on this listing is outdated and incomplete.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Logan County School Employees Federal Credit Union serves school employees in Logan, WV with savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans.
